majorEmployers.name: Jonell Systems
majorEmployers.notes: Capital headquarters for global filtration systems company serving oil and gas industry. ISO 9001 certified.

majorEmployers.name: Ascension St. John Nowata
majorEmployers.notes: 24-hour hospital and medical center serving Nowata County and surrounding communities.

majorEmployers.notes: District serves approximately 745 students in PK-12 at 3 schools.

uniqueFacts: The name Nowata comes from a Delaware Indian word no-we-ata meaning come here or welcome, though a popular legend claims it originated from a sign reading No Water posted at a dry spring.
Nowata County was part of the Cherokee Nations Cooweescoowee District before Oklahoma statehood in 1907.
The 1904 discovery of oil and gas made Nowata County home to the Cherokee Shallow Sand District, once known as the worlds largest shallow oil field.
The Nowata County Courthouse, built in 1912, is listed on the National Register of Historic Places and is the only property in the county with that designation.
Chief Charles Journeycake of the Delaware Tribe purchased the land where Nowata now sits from the Cherokee Nation in 1868.
processServingNotes: The Nowata County Courthouse houses both the District Court Clerk and the County Clerk in the same building at 229 N. Maple St. The Court Clerk handles court filings while the County Clerk handles land records.
Nowata County has municipal courts in Nowata, South Coffeyville, and Delaware that handle city violations separate from the District Court docket. Verify which court has jurisdiction before serving.
The Nowata County Jail is located adjacent to the courthouse at the same address. Inmate service may be coordinated through the sheriffs office at (918) 273-2287.
heroLead: Nestled along the Kansas border in northeastern Oklahoma, Nowata County carries a Delaware Indian name meaning welcome and was once home to the worlds largest shallow oil field. Today the countys economy blends agriculture, manufacturing, and Cherokee Nation enterprises across its six small towns. For reliable process serving throughout Nowata County, call Just Legal Solutions at (539) 367-6832 or visit our /contact page.

faqs.question: Where is the Nowata County Courthouse located and what are the hours?
faqs.answer: The Nowata County Courthouse is located at 229 N. Maple St., Nowata, OK 74048. The Court Clerk office is open Monday through Friday from 8:00 AM to 4:30 PM and can be reached at (918) 273-0127. The courthouse sits just off Highway 169, about 50 miles north of Tulsa, near the Kansas-Oklahoma border.

faqs.question: What judicial district is Nowata County in and who are the judges?
faqs.answer: Nowata County is part of the 11th Judicial District, which also includes Washington County and Bartlesville. The presiding District Judge is Linda Thomas, who hears cases in both counties. The Associate District Judge for Nowata County is Carl Gibson, who primarily presides over the daily 9:00 AM docket and remains in Nowata County. For process serving questions in Nowata County, call Just Legal Solutions at (539) 367-6832.

faqs.question: Does Cherokee Nation jurisdiction affect process serving in Nowata County?
faqs.answer: Yes, Nowata County falls within the Cherokee Nation reservation boundaries. Cherokee Nation exercises tribal jurisdiction over the area, which may affect certain legal proceedings involving tribal members or matters under tribal law. Process servers should be aware that tribal courts operate separately from state district courts.

faqs.question: What are the largest employers in Nowata County?
faqs.answer: Major employers include Jonell Systems, a filtration equipment manufacturer with its capital headquarters in Nowata; Ascension St. John Nowata hospital on South Locust Street; Nowata Public Schools serving approximately 745 students; Disan Engineering Corporation, a woman-owned military electronics manufacturer; and Cherokee Casino South Coffeyville operated by Cherokee Nation Entertainment.

faqs.question: What towns and cities are in Nowata County?
faqs.answer: Nowata County has six incorporated municipalities: Nowata (the county seat), South Coffeyville, Delaware, Lenapah, Wann, and New Alluwe. Nowata is the largest community with approximately 3,500 residents. The county also contains unincorporated communities including Watova and Childers. When serving process, verify the correct municipal or district court for each location.

faqs.question: What is the history behind Nowata Countys name?
faqs.answer: Nowata derives from the Delaware Indian word no-we-ata, meaning come here or welcome. The name was suggested by a Delaware woman to railroad surveyors in 1889, though officials in Washington misspelled it as Nowata on official documents. An alternative legend claims a traveler posted a No Water sign at dry springs. The county was named after the town when both were established at Oklahoma statehood in 1907. For help with process serving in Nowata County, contact Just Legal Solutions at (539) 367-6832.
